summary: Four-section change-impact map for embedding `crates/norn` in a Tauri 2 macOS+iOS app. The intended in-process driver surface is already substantial (`AgentBuilder` → `Agent`/`AgentHandle` or `Agent::into_parts` → public `run_agent_step`), but stateful multi-turn policy remains duplicated in CLI/TUI drivers. The highest-priority iOS blocker is not subprocess tooling: `DescriptorGovernor::global()` currently cannot initialize on iOS because open-FD counting is implemented only for macOS/Linux, while ordinary provider HTTP and filesystem operations require that governor. After that P0, arbitrary process launch, desktop OAuth, home-directory storage, and mobile lifecycle durability need explicit platform seams. Observed facts and inferred change impact are separated in each section below.

map.area: 1. REUSE AS-IS — public embedding surface and visibility gaps
map.responsibility: The current native-library contract a GUI can embed directly, plus the boundaries that are private, non-serializable, or missing as a unified public service.
map.evidence:
OBSERVED — crate facade: `crates/norn/src/lib.rs:3-24` makes agent/config/context/integration/process/provider/session/skill/tool modules public; `lib.rs:29-38` deliberately re-exports the crate-private `r#loop` as public `agent_loop`. Build/run: `agent/builder.rs:80-85` defines `AgentBuilder` as the in-process front door; `agent/builder/init.rs:13-67` shows `new(Arc<dyn Provider>)` and that every other input is optional; public setters cover working/workspace dirs, tools, schema/config/retry/session/channels/hooks/registry/variables/extensions (`agent/builder_setters.rs:139-212`, `225-303`, `327-501`, `544-667`). `Agent::handle` is public and cloneable (`agent/instance.rs:179-192`); one-shot `Agent::run` executes the canonical step (`agent/instance.rs:234-305`).
OBSERVED — custom-driver contract: `Agent::into_parts` and every `AgentParts` payload field are public (`agent/instance.rs:55-117`, `144-177`), including provider, generation-leased tool runtime, MCP control, `LoopContext`, effective config/model/tool definitions, live `Arc<EventStore>`, event sender/channel, cancellation token, inbound receiver/sender, identity, session entry, and replay summary. Session start/end helpers are public (`instance.rs:119-141`). The step request and both prompt/message entry points are public (`loop/runner/mod.rs:35-42`), so a GUI can invoke `run_agent_step` or `run_agent_step_from_messages` without a CLI subprocess.
OBSERVED — live events/IPC: `.event_channel_capacity` builds the shared broadcast and exposes subscriptions (`agent/builder_setters.rs:372-391`); `AgentHandle::subscribe` documents lag and lifetime (`agent/handle.rs:156-174`). `AgentEventKind` covers provider, child lifecycle, inter-agent messages, usage estimates, stream retries, and compaction (`provider/agent_event.rs:406-430`); `AgentEvent` includes agent id/role (`agent_event.rs:432-448`). GAP: `AgentEventKind`, `AgentEvent`, and `ProviderEvent` derive `Clone, Debug` but not serde (`agent_event.rs:409-410`, `439-440`; `provider/events.rs:29-33`), so Tauri IPC needs a GUI-owned DTO/projection or new serializable event schema.
OBSERVED — steering/cancellation: `.inbound_capacity` wires a bounded receiver drained at tool boundaries and exposes a sender (`agent/builder_setters.rs:408-440`); `InboundChannel` is public and documents that steering never interrupts streaming/running tools (`loop/inbound.rs:1-16`, `158-166`), with public drain/wake APIs (`inbound.rs:188-247`). `.cancel_token` links an embedder token (`builder_setters.rs:394-405`); `AgentHandle::cancel` and `cancellation_token` are public (`agent/handle.rs:136-154`) and cancellation is observed mid-provider-stream or at iteration boundaries, while an executing tool finishes (`agent/handle.rs:53-62`).
OBSERVED — children: `AgentRegistry::shared` plus snapshots/status/tombstones are public (`agent/registry.rs:158-190`, `192-268`); builder coordination requires explicit registry, child policy, and child-result capacity rather than hidden defaults (`agent/builder_setters.rs:490-514`, `534-557`). Child lifecycle and message events share the root broadcast (`provider/agent_event.rs:23-37`, `406-430`).
OBSERVED — sessions/action log: `SessionManager` is public and explicitly accepts an embedder-owned directory (`session/manager.rs:104-140`, `160-190`). Public lifecycle methods are create/resume/open-or-resume (`session/manager/open.rs:28-70`, `73-140`), fork (`session/manager/fork.rs:24-88`), list/resolve (`session/manager.rs:217-239`), and delete (`session/manager.rs:333`); every open returns sink-equipped `OpenSession { store, entry, replay }` (`session/manager.rs:85-102`). `AgentBuilder::open_session` carries manager/spec/durability into the assembled agent (`agent/builder_setters.rs:332-370`). `ActionLog` and query/record methods are public (`session/mod.rs:36-39`; `session/action_log.rs:344`, `380-431`, `514-677`) and the assembled `LoopContext::action_log` is a public field (`loop/loop_context.rs:203-217`).
OBSERVED — hooks/skills/MCP: programmatic hook traits and `HookRegistry` are public (`integration/hooks/mod.rs:33-48`), and `.hooks` accepts an `Arc<HookRegistry>` (`agent/builder_setters.rs:451-455`). Skill discovery/catalog/template APIs are public (`skill/mod.rs:13-27`). MCP client/runtime/control types are exported (`integration/mod.rs:44-63`); builders can attach an existing runtime, layered state, or selected-server view (`agent/mcp.rs:113-141`), and `AgentHandle::mcp_control` is public (`agent/handle.rs:187-191`).
OBSERVED — provider/auth: direct public constructors exist for `OpenAiProvider::new(ProviderConfig)` (`provider/openai/provider.rs:37,69-72`) and `OpenAiCompatibleProvider::new` (`provider/openai_compatible/provider.rs:23,50-54`); `ProviderConfig` publicly carries auth/base URL/timeouts/retries/options/debug dump/rate limits (`provider/request.rs:299-360`). Provider/account exports include auth source construction, login/logout/list/use (`provider/mod.rs:12-16`); named login/list/use are public (`provider/auth/accounts.rs:18-21`, `235-245`); OAuth account types are public (`provider/openai_oauth/mod.rs:39-72`).
VISIBILITY/SHAPE GAPS — intended assembly internals remain private (`agent/mod.rs:5-7`, `29-34`), including `registry_assembly`, `session_open`, and the private MCP attachment/runtime wrapper (`agent/mcp.rs:16-25`); normal embedders do not need them because `AgentBuilder`/`AgentParts` surface the result. The root schedule/process arming functions are `pub(crate)` (`agent/arming.rs:348-355`, `409-428`), so a driver bypassing `AgentBuilder` cannot reproduce fully aligned assembly. Mobile stop/partial event constants are also `pub(crate)` (`loop/stop_records.rs:42-48`). There is no library-level provider-kind + merged-settings factory: concrete constructors are public, but the only unified factory is in the reference CLI (`crates/norn-cli/src/print/provider.rs:134-152`).

map.area: 2. DRIVER-DUPLICATED LOGIC — candidates to lift from norn-cli/norn-tui
map.responsibility: Execution-mechanism policy currently implemented separately by print/TUI drivers that a third native GUI driver would otherwise have to reconstruct.
map.evidence:
OBSERVED — slash authority is split: the library owns command metadata/parsers but explicitly labels `BuiltinSlashCommand::kind` as the side-effecting handler selected by each UI adapter (`crates/norn/src/loop/commands.rs:31-38`, `40-82`, `107-138`). The TUI maps `/new` and `/clear` to local `handle_new` and `/compact` to `handle_compact` (`crates/norn-tui/src/app/slash.rs:62-95`); persistent `/new` independently creates a `SessionManager`, derives cwd, chooses `DurabilityPolicy::Flush`, and constructs a new `SessionBinding` (`slash.rs:147-196`). Its commit updates context edits, store dependencies, cache key, prompt environment session id, display id, and usage (`slash.rs:223-297`). `/compact` separately estimates, mutates `ContextEdits`, and checkpoints (`slash.rs:300-355`). Print mode has another implementation: `apply_compact_request` (`crates/norn-cli/src/commands/slash/actions.rs:72-115`) and `/clear` session rotation (`actions.rs:118-199`). REQUIRED FOR CONSISTENCY: lift stateful slash actions/session rotation into a library multi-turn driver/service; rendering remains UI-specific.
OBSERVED — rotation is nontrivial duplicated authority: TUI `rotate_store_dependents` must checkpoint the old store, rebuild/replay `ActionLog`, replace shared `ToolContext` entries and `ActionLogTree`, rebuild `AgentToolInfra` with the new store/session binding, then swap loop/store slots (`crates/norn-tui/src/app/rotation.rs:1-31`, `42-90`, `92-175`). This is a strong library-lift candidate; a third implementation is likely to leave children or action-log queries attached to the retired session.
OBSERVED — multi-turn orchestration is UI-owned: TUI `TuiInputs` carries almost all `AgentParts` fields (`app/event_loop.rs:52-109`); `run_app` owns initial prompt and outer event loop (`event_loop.rs:115-218`). `run_turn_and_pending` sequences user turn, follow-ups, and child prompts (`app/turn/run.rs:47-85`); each turn creates a new cancellation token/active-input channel, calls public step APIs, selects concurrently over cancellation, active input, child results, `AgentEvent`, and render ticks, drains remaining events, repairs tool results on cancellation, checkpoints, and finalizes (`turn/run.rs:195-250`, `253-381`). A GUI must currently repeat the non-rendering portions.
OBSERVED — print mode repeats a one-turn variant: it dispatches slash actions, mutates model/schema/effort, fires hooks, creates renderer/emitter/intervention tasks, converts the tool generation store to `Arc<dyn ToolExecutor>`, builds `AgentStepRequest`, runs it, and checkpoints (`crates/norn-cli/src/print/orchestrator.rs:374-510`, `515-577`; end hook at `:688`; checkpoint at `:697-757`). `AgentParts::fire_session_start/end` reduce hook drift, but orchestration remains driver-owned.
OBSERVED — provider/settings factory is CLI-only: settings and provider-profile overlays are folded at `crates/norn-cli/src/config/overrides/provider.rs:9-40`, fields are overlaid at `:42-90`, and CLI overrides win at `:93-138`. `build_provider` then resolves auth/account rules and desktop defaults, constructs `ProviderConfig`, and chooses OpenAI/OpenAI-compatible/Claude Runner (`crates/norn-cli/src/print/provider.rs:134-195`, `197-250`). A native GUI can call public constructors but must currently duplicate precedence/default/account validation.
OBSERVED — debug/startup infrastructure is driver-owned: both TUI and print derive debug dump filenames before the session id exists (`crates/norn-cli/src/tui/driver.rs:96-108`; `print/orchestrator.rs:206-220`). TUI startup milestones are a private CLI type (`tui/startup_trace.rs:5-40`, `62-90`) and are manually marked around LSP/invocation/provider/MCP/build/session/handoff (`tui/driver.rs:80-133`, `174-204`, `237-280`).
OBSERVED — NOFILE startup is CLI-only: `norn-cli/src/main.rs:17-23` invokes it before parsing; `norn-cli/src/nofile.rs:47-53` explicitly says library embedders never call it implicitly, and Unix mutation is at `nofile.rs:125-148`. INFERENCE: do not transplant this to iOS; if retained for macOS GUI, make it a platform startup capability rather than another ad hoc driver step. PLAUSIBLE BUT UNPROVEN COLLATERAL: input history, TTY rendering, JSON-RPC framing, and status-panel view logic are presentation/protocol concerns and need not move into `norn`.

map.area: 3. iOS BREAKAGE AUDIT — desktop Unix assumptions and gating strategy
map.responsibility: Compile/runtime audit of process launch, descriptor governance, OAuth, credential/path storage, Unix cfgs, watchers, and TTY assumptions on iOS, with proposed gates.
map.evidence:
P0 OBSERVED HARD BLOCKER — descriptor admission currently disables even network-only operation on iOS. `current_limits` uses `#[cfg(unix)]` and `getrlimit(Nofile)` (`resource/descriptor.rs:137-147`), so iOS enters that branch; but open-FD counting supports only `target_os = macos` `/dev/fd` or Linux `/proc/self/fd` and returns `Unsupported` on every other target (`descriptor.rs:157-182`). `DescriptorGovernor::global` requires both limits and open count (`resource/descriptor_governor.rs:91-117`), so iOS initialization returns `DescriptorAdmissionError`. Ordinary provider requests acquire this governor before `reqwest::send` (`provider/exec.rs:160-181`), and ordinary/private filesystem and recursive walks do too (`resource/admission.rs:9-44`, HTTP wrapper `:79-98`). CONSEQUENCE: OpenAI streams, OAuth HTTP, session/auth private-file operations, and filesystem tools fail before subprocess exclusion matters. REQUIRED FIX: add an iOS-safe descriptor-observation/admission backend or inject an embedder-supplied bounded capacity; do not fake open count as zero. `DescriptorGovernor` itself is `pub(crate)` (`descriptor_governor.rs:84-98`), so configuration also needs a public initialization seam. NOFILE mutation is not in the library; CLI-only `setrlimit` is at `crates/norn-cli/src/nofile.rs:125-148` and must stay macOS/desktop-gated.
P0 OBSERVED — arbitrary executable launch is pervasive and unavailable to sandboxed iOS apps (no general child-process/fork-exec entitlement). Exact production paths: foreground bash uses `Command::new("sh").arg("-c")` and Unix process groups (`tools/bash/process.rs:234-257`, `260-282`); background manager does the same and spools pipes (`process/manager/launch.rs:187-258`, `335-356`); process-output watches execute each filter with `sh -c` (`process/watch_exec.rs:190-236`); config shell hooks (`integration/hooks/shell.rs:166-231`); shell variables (`integration/variables.rs:286-330`); per-request prompt commands (`loop/loop_context/prompt_context.rs:428-466`); shell-backed rules (`rules/engine.rs:166-203`); skill template shell expansion (`skill/template.rs:348-394`); Rhai `run_cmd` (`integration/rhai/blocking.rs:52-76`); stdio extensions (`integration/extensions.rs:162-233`); stdio MCP servers (`integration/mcp_stdio.rs:93-178`); and Claude Runner invokes an external CLI (`integration/claude/adapter.rs:84-130`). `watch_exec` is a process-output watcher, not an OS filesystem watcher.
P0 GATING — create a compile feature such as `desktop-process` covering Tokio `process`, `claude_runner`, bash/process/watch, stdio MCP/extensions, Claude integration, and shell execution helpers. Today features only contain `test-utils`/`libyggd-ast` (`crates/norn/Cargo.toml:8-10`), while `claude_runner`, `libc`, `rustix`, Tokio process-capable workspace features, and `tiny_http` are unconditional (`crates/norn/Cargo.toml:12-64`; workspace Tokio enables `process/net/fs/signal` at `Cargo.toml:39-50`). Runtime gating is also required on macOS sandbox profiles: return typed `UnsupportedCapability`, reject shell-backed profiles/hooks/rules/variables at assembly, and omit incompatible tools. Tool-name exclusion already exists: the standard set registers bash and other tools (`tools/registry_builder.rs:34-74`), builder additionally registers `cron` and `process` then applies `without_tools` (`agent/registry_assembly.rs:41-70`). However, `.without_tools(["bash","process","lsp",...])` does not disable prompt commands, shell variables/rules/hooks/skills/Rhai, so a single runtime capability policy must cover all indirect launch paths. HTTP MCP is the viable mobile profile: the MCP client explicitly supports stdio or HTTP (`integration/mcp_client.rs:1-7`) and transport dispatch chooses stdio vs HTTP (`mcp_client.rs:224-235`).
OBSERVED — Unix cfgs are too broad for iOS process behavior. Process-manager and bash process-group code uses `#[cfg(unix)]` (`process/manager/launch.rs:152-160`, `349-350`; `tools/bash/process.rs:250-251`, `269-282`), which includes iOS even though launch is unusable. Gate on the desktop-process capability/target, not `unix`. Private descriptor-relative filesystem operations also select the broad Unix implementation (`util/private_fs.rs:143-178`, `187-255`, `268-370`); these may work inside the app container, but require an iOS target test and must receive only sandbox-authorized absolute roots. This is plausible collateral, not a demonstrated failure after the descriptor-governor fix.
P0/P1 OBSERVED — browser OAuth cannot work as written on iOS. Browser launch is a supervised child process (`provider/openai_oauth/browser.rs:82-175`); macOS specifically invokes `/usr/bin/osascript` (`browser.rs:347-380`), and all unlisted targets—including iOS—return “opening a browser is unsupported” (`browser.rs:394-405`). Browser login also binds localhost ports 1455/1457 and builds an `http://localhost` redirect (`provider/openai_oauth/login_server.rs:44-49`, `211-249`). iOS needs `ASWebAuthenticationSession` (or a Tauri plugin/native bridge) and app/universal-link callback handling, not process launch plus a desktop loopback listener. Strategy: inject a public `AuthorizationSession` abstraction; keep current desktop implementation behind `desktop-oauth`, add iOS implementation on the main/UI side. Device-code login exists (`provider/auth/login.rs:13-25`, `60-72`) but currently requires an explicit presenter described as terminal-only (`provider/auth/accounts.rs:42-53`; `provider/openai_oauth/login_prompt.rs:57-68`); generalize that trusted presenter to a GUI secret-presentation sink if device code is the fallback.
P1 OBSERVED — credential storage is file-only, not Keychain/keyring. `AuthCredentialsStoreMode` has only `File`, defaulting to `$NORN_HOME/auth/auth.json` (`provider/openai_oauth/storage.rs:18-26`); OAuth provider construction hardcodes `File` (`provider/auth.rs:153-172`) and browser/device account login also hardcodes it (`provider/auth/accounts.rs:151-179`). There is no `keyring` dependency in `crates/norn/Cargo.toml:12-64`. File storage can be made container-local, but mobile requires a credential-store trait and iOS Keychain implementation (with migration/export policy); do not rely on `~/.norn/auth` or normal backup semantics for long-lived refresh tokens.
P1 OBSERVED — home/cwd assumptions need explicit app paths. `norn_dir` reads process-global `NORN_HOME`, else `dirs::home_dir()/.norn` (`config/paths.rs:53-70`); standard sessions resolve the same authority (`config/paths.rs:72-115`, `192-235`; `session/manager/standard.rs:5-20`). `AgentBuilder` falls back to and canonicalizes process cwd (`agent/assembly/paths.rs:10-40`). iOS storage is an isolated app container and cannot share the Mac CLI’s `~/.norn`; a Tauri driver should always pass an app-data/support directory to `SessionManager::new` (explicit embedder ownership is documented at `session/manager.rs:104-109`) and explicit sandbox-authorized working/workspace roots. macOS may deliberately use `SessionManager::standard()` to share CLI sessions or a separate app store; this is a product choice.
OBSERVED — no library TTY requirement and no filesystem watcher to port. The source-wide TTY search found no `IsTerminal`/terminal backend in `crates/norn`; TTY setup lives in the reference TUI (`crates/norn-tui/src/app/event_loop.rs:115-163`). OAuth’s presenter is semantically terminal-named as above, which should be generalized. Mutation tracking explicitly says “No filesystem watcher, no polling” (`session/mutation_ledger.rs:13-16`). MCP “watchers” are protocol tool-list notification tasks, while process watches are shell filters; neither is an OS file watcher.

map.area: 4. MOBILE-CRITICAL GAPS — lifecycle, recovery, storage, and wake deltas
map.responsibility: Missing APIs and durable state transitions required when an iOS app backgrounds, loses connectivity, is killed, resumes in another process, or is awakened externally.
map.evidence:
SUSPEND/RESUME GAP — OBSERVED: `Agent` is single-use and `run` consumes it (`agent/instance.rs:32-35`, `234-250`); the only lifecycle control is cooperative cancellation (`agent/handle.rs:136-154`), which can interrupt a provider stream but waits for a running tool (`agent/handle.rs:53-62`). There is no durable pause token or API that rehydrates an in-progress `LoopContext`/tool execution. INFERENCE/DELTA: add a mobile lifecycle coordinator around a library-owned multi-turn driver: on background-expiration, stop accepting new tools, cooperatively cancel the provider stream, append/checkpoint a typed suspended/interrupted-turn record, and return a resumable session/turn descriptor. Resumption normally must replay/reissue from a durable request boundary; an arbitrary SSE stream cannot simply continue after process suspension.
PARTIAL-TURN KILL GAP — NEAREST MACHINERY: provider deltas are assembled in memory, and the complete `AssistantMessage` is persisted only after response assembly/post-LLM hooks (`loop/runner/provider_call.rs:83-107`, `112-160`, `233-272`). `TimeoutState::in_flight_partial` is explicitly content “not yet attached to a durable assistant turn” (`loop/timeout_state.rs:9-22`, `35-50`). On cooperative timeout/cancel, `record_abnormal_step_stop` best-effort appends `loop.partial_output` then `loop.step_stopped` (`loop/stop_records.rs:1-30`, `68-137`, `139-173`), but those records are replay-only and take no recovery action (`stop_records.rs:12-18`). An iOS SIGKILL/termination cannot run this cleanup, so all deltas since the last durable event are lost. DELTA: introduce a write-ahead in-flight turn/attempt record before the request and bounded incremental checkpoints (or a durable delta sidecar), then a commit marker when `AssistantMessage` lands; resume must detect an uncommitted attempt and expose recover/retry/discard policy. Expose typed event constants/schema now hidden at `stop_records.rs:42-48`.
NETWORK-CHANGE GAP — NEAREST MACHINERY: default retry covers network timeout, reset, and 5xx with only two retries and 1s/2s backoff (`loop/retry.rs:20-25`, `39-61`, `64-101`); `retry_with_backoff` sleeps and exhausts this fixed budget without reachability/lifecycle input (`retry.rs:164-211`). DELTA: inject a connectivity/reachability signal into retry orchestration, pause retry-budget consumption while offline/backgrounded, classify resumable stream disconnects, and resume/reissue when the OS reports a viable path. Preserve `AgentEventKind::StreamRetry` semantics so UI partial output resets correctly (`provider/agent_event.rs:424-427`).
SESSION-STORE PORTABILITY GAP — NEAREST MACHINERY: `EventStore` writes through a pluggable `PersistenceSink` before publishing in memory (`session/store.rs:23-70`) and exposes checkpoint semantics (`store.rs:104-113`); durability choices include flush versus per-event/every-N fsync (`session/jsonl_sink.rs:30-43`). `SessionManager::new` already accepts an arbitrary app-owned directory (`session/manager.rs:104-109`, `160-190`). DELTA: define platform storage selection and migration/export/import at the GUI boundary: macOS may opt into the standard `~/.norn/session-store`, whereas iOS must use its container and cannot transparently share with CLI (`config/paths.rs:192-235`). Choose `FsyncPerEvent` or explicit background-transition checkpoints for kill-sensitive mobile sessions; `Flush` does not issue fsync (`jsonl_sink.rs:36-42`).
PUSH/WAKE GAP — NEAREST MACHINERY: durable pending inter-agent messages use queued/dequeued custom events and replay (`agent/pending_messages.rs:1-16`), expose FIFO observations (`pending_messages.rs:79-104`, `181-201`), and can rebuild from events (`agent/pending_replay.rs:79-97`). In-session schedules rebuild on resume and fire past-due one-shots late, but live timers “die with the process; only the event record survives” (`agent/arming.rs:330-346`). There is no APNs/background-task registration or external wake adapter. DELTA: add a host-provided wake scheduler/push ingress that stores an idempotent wake/message in the target session before asking iOS for execution; when launched, `open_or_resume` and pending replay drain it. iOS may not grant immediate/background execution, so delivery must remain queued until the next allowed launch.
CHILD-LIFECYCLE COLLATERAL — OBSERVED: `AgentRegistry` is an in-memory `HashMap`/path index/tombstone set shared through `Arc<RwLock<_>>` (`agent/registry.rs:158-190`); process-manager state is explicitly empty after resume (`agent/arming.rs:393-405`). INFERENCE: process death loses live child controllers/registry status even though child session events may survive. A mobile recovery design must mark previously-running agents interrupted/unknown from durable lifecycle events rather than pretending they are still live; full child-task continuation is not currently available.

entry_points: `norn::provider::{openai::OpenAiProvider,openai_compatible::OpenAiCompatibleProvider}` and OAuth account functions — constructors at `provider/openai/provider.rs:69-72`, `provider/openai_compatible/provider.rs:50-54`; exports at `provider/mod.rs:12-16`.

Reference only: CLI/TUI custom-driver assembly begins at `crates/norn-cli/src/tui/driver.rs:79-176`, then hands `TuiInputs` to `norn_tui::run_app` at `driver.rs:251-282`; print assembly/orchestration begins at `crates/norn-cli/src/print/orchestrator.rs:184-250,344-380`.
dependencies: Workspace Tokio unconditionally enables `rt-multi-thread`, `process`, `net`, `fs`, and `signal` (`Cargo.toml:39-50`); a mobile build should split process/signal features from core async/network needs.

`crates/norn` unconditionally depends on `claude_runner`, `libc`, `rustix`, `dirs`, `rhai`, `tiny_http`, and Tokio io-std (`crates/norn/Cargo.toml:12-64`); platform features are currently only `test-utils` and `libyggd-ast` (`:8-10`).
Provider HTTP uses reqwest/rustls (`Cargo.toml:80-87`) but every send is additionally gated by the library descriptor governor (`crates/norn/src/provider/exec.rs:175-181`).

Session/auth persistence depends on descriptor-relative `rustix` filesystem operations (`crates/norn/src/util/private_fs.rs:143-255,268-370`) and a functioning descriptor governor (`resource/admission.rs:9-32`).
MCP has two distinct dependency profiles: stdio child process (`integration/mcp_stdio.rs:93-178`) versus reqwest HTTP (`integration/mcp_client.rs:224-235`).

The `lsp`, `diagnostics`, and `claude_runner` dependencies are git revisions (`Cargo.toml:17-21`); their own iOS target support was not established by this source-only audit and needs separate transitive review.
risks: P0: on iOS `DescriptorGovernor::global()` lacks an open-FD observation (`resource/descriptor.rs:157-165`; `descriptor_governor.rs:107-117`), causing provider HTTP and private filesystem admission to fail before useful execution.

P0: default `AgentBuilder` tool assembly includes `bash` and `process` (`tools/registry_builder.rs:61-74`; `agent/registry_assembly.rs:53-70`), while iOS cannot spawn arbitrary executables; a missed mobile profile would expose tools that always fail.

P0: shell execution is indirect as well as tool-driven—hooks, variables, prompt commands, rules, skills, Rhai, MCP stdio, extensions, and Claude Runner each have independent `Command` paths cited in section 3. Tool-name filtering alone is insufficient.

P0/P1: browser OAuth is explicitly unsupported on iOS (`provider/openai_oauth/browser.rs:394-405`), and the loopback callback model (`login_server.rs:211-249`) does not provide an `ASWebAuthenticationSession` bridge.
P1 security: OAuth storage has only file mode (`provider/openai_oauth/storage.rs:21-26`) and constructors hardcode it (`provider/auth.rs:168-172`); there is no Keychain-backed credential abstraction.

P1 durability: streamed assistant output remains only in memory until response publication (`loop/runner/provider_call.rs:83-107,270-281`); cooperative cancellation records partials, abrupt app termination does not.

P1 consistency: session rotation rewires at least store, action log, action-log tree, child infra/session binding, cache key, and prompt environment (`norn-tui/src/app/rotation.rs:42-175`; `app/slash.rs:254-279`). A GUI-local reimplementation can silently fork/query the retired session.

P1 observability: `AgentEvent`/`ProviderEvent` are not serde types (`provider/agent_event.rs:409-440`; `provider/events.rs:29-33`), so direct Tauri event emission is not available without a stable projection.
Unverified compile risk: this audit obeyed the request not to run Cargo, so iOS compilation of rustix, tree-sitter grammars, tokei, reqwest/rustls, and git dependencies remains unproven.
questions: Storage policy decision: should the macOS GUI intentionally share CLI `~/.norn/session-store` (`config/paths.rs:192-235`) or use an app-owned store like iOS? If shared, define multi-process locking, account, and migration UX.

Auth decision: use native `ASWebAuthenticationSession` + Keychain as primary, or expose device-code flow through a generalized trusted GUI presenter (`provider/openai_oauth/login_prompt.rs:57-68`) as the first mobile implementation?

Capability decision: exact mobile tool profile. Minimum likely excludes bash/process/watch, LSP-backed process launch, stdio MCP/extensions, Claude Runner, shell hooks/variables/rules/prompt commands/skill shell/Rhai `run_cmd`; decide whether app-container read/write/edit/search and HTTP MCP/web tools remain enabled.

Lifecycle decision: when iOS backgrounds mid-turn, should Norn cooperatively cancel and persist an interrupted turn, request bounded background time to finish, or both in sequence? Existing API supports cancel, not pause (`agent/handle.rs:136-154`).

Wake decision: which host service owns APNs/BGTask scheduling and durable push payload storage? Norn’s current schedule executor is process-local and only re-arms from events (`agent/arming.rs:330-346`).
recommended_next_steps: P0 — Make descriptor admission target-portable first: add an iOS observation/injected-budget implementation and a public one-time initialization seam; add focused tests proving provider HTTP and private filesystem permits can be acquired without `/dev/fd` or `/proc/self/fd` (`resource/descriptor.rs:157-182`; `descriptor_governor.rs:91-117`).

P0 — Add Cargo/platform features: `core`, `desktop-process`, `desktop-oauth`, optionally `lsp/claude-runner`. Gate modules and dependencies, not just tool registration. Provide typed unsupported-capability stubs where API stability matters.

P0 — Add a public runtime capability policy consumed by AgentBuilder assembly. It must omit tools and reject/disable every indirect shell source; use `.without_tools` only as the existing low-level tool gate (`agent/registry_assembly.rs:53-70`).

P0 — Lift a library `MultiTurnDriver`/`AgentSession` service around `AgentParts`: submit prompt/messages, stream subscribed events, steer/cancel, checkpoint, execute compact/clear/new, and rotate all store dependents atomically. Keep UI rendering and Tauri IPC adapters outside.
P1 — Define serializable, versioned GUI event DTOs for `AgentEvent`/`ProviderEvent`, including lag/retry/reset semantics, rather than exposing unstable debug formatting.

P1 — Introduce platform auth abstractions: native authorization session and credential store. Keep current osascript+loopback+file implementation as desktop; implement ASWebAuthenticationSession+Keychain for iOS; retain public account catalog operations above the storage backend.

P1 — Introduce durable in-flight turn state: begin-attempt WAL, bounded delta checkpoints, response commit marker, interrupted-attempt recovery policy, and a background-transition `checkpoint_and_suspend` API. Reuse `EventStore` sinks and `SessionManager::open_or_resume`.

P1 — Add connectivity-aware retry input so offline/background periods do not consume the fixed retry budget; retain existing typed retry classification and stream-reset event behavior (`loop/retry.rs:39-101,164-211`; `provider/agent_event.rs:424-427`).

P1 — Have the Tauri host always supply explicit app data and working roots on iOS; define export/import/sync instead of assuming CLI-home sharing. Choose and document `FsyncPerEvent` versus transition checkpoints (`session/jsonl_sink.rs:30-43`).

P2 — Add CI target checks for `aarch64-apple-ios` and simulator plus source-level feature-matrix tests. Separately audit git/transitive crates (`lsp`, `diagnostics`, `claude_runner`) before claiming iOS support.
verification: Read-only repository scout: no files edited, no git state mutated, and no Cargo commands run, per task constraint.

Public-surface verification used crate/module declarations (`crates/norn/src/lib.rs:3-38`, `agent/mod.rs:5-72`, `integration/mod.rs:6-67`, `session/mod.rs:6-92`, `provider/mod.rs:3-69`) plus concrete reference-consumer call paths, not design filenames alone.

Subprocess audit searched all `crates/norn/src/**/*.rs` for `Command::new`, `std::process`, and `tokio::process`; production hits were traced in section 3. Test-only child processes were excluded from conclusions.

Platform audit searched all `#[cfg(unix)]`/target-os branches. The decisive iOS path was followed from `descriptor.rs:137-165` → `descriptor_governor.rs:91-117` → provider send `provider/exec.rs:175-181` and filesystem admission `resource/admission.rs:9-32`.

TTY/file-watch audit searched `crates/norn` for terminal APIs and watcher crates. No library TTY backend was found; TTY ownership was confirmed in `norn-tui` (`app/event_loop.rs:115-163`). The library explicitly states no filesystem watcher/polling for mutation tracking (`session/mutation_ledger.rs:13-16`).

Negative limitation: without the requested Cargo target check, compile-time support of the full dependency graph on iOS remains unknown; recommendations distinguish observed source failures from this unproven collateral.
